findings.title: No-rate-currency settle silently fabricates a 1:1 euroValue — corrupts EUR/Global/Insights balances (or hard-blocks settling a real exotic-currency debt)
findings.rootCause: settleDebt (actions.ts:115) unconditionally computes `euroValue = convertCurrency(amount, currency, 'EUR')`. For a no-rate currency, convertCurrency → _rateFor (currency.ts:71-78) falls through to `CONVERSION_RATES[upper] || 1` = 1 on BOTH sides, so it returns the raw foreign amount unchanged (50000 ARS → €50000). It then always spreads that fabricated euroValue into the POST. Server create_settlement only rejects a no-rate non-EUR settlement when `rate is None AND euro_value is None` (settlements.py:222-228); because the client always sends a (wrong) euroValue, that gate never fires, and compute_euro_value's cold path (fx_rates.py:228) just returns the client hint verbatim. The expense form has the correct pattern — `needsManualEuro = !!selCurrency && !hasRate(selCurrency)` (ManualTab.tsx:108) forces an explicit EUR amount for no-rate currencies — but the settlement modal and the one-click Settle button have NO such gate. The suggested-debt Settle button (SettlementView.tsx:310-320) renders for every currency gated only on tripIsEditable; `hasRate(d.currency)` at line 302 controls only the cosmetic '≈ EUR' hint, not the button.

findings.title: Settlement History groups rows by UTC calendar date — evening settlements show under the wrong day for non-UTC users, and same-day rows have unstable ordering
findings.rootCause: collectSettlementHistory (viewData.ts:178) derives the grouping/sort key as `date: (s.createdAt || '').slice(0,10)`. createdAt comes from settlements.created_at, which is SQLite `DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` = UTC (baseline.py:45). Slicing the first 10 chars yields the UTC calendar date, not the viewer's local date. The History tab then compares that key against `new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` (also UTC) for Today/Yesterday labels (SettlementView.tsx:370-373) and sorts by `new Date(b.date) - new Date(a.date)` (viewData.ts:184), which—because the time component was dropped—cannot order same-day rows.

findings.title: Hardcoded English 'Couldn't undo' toast on settlement-delete failure (i18n gap)
findings.rootCause: deleteSettlement's failure branch builds the message as a raw template literal: `Couldn't undo: ${result.error || 'Network error'}` (actions.ts:261) instead of going through t(). The codebase already has the localized sibling `settlement.toastSettlementFailed: 'Settlement failed: {error}'` (en.ts:651, mirrored across locales) used on the create path — the delete path simply wasn't wired to an equivalent key. Because it's a hardcoded literal it bypasses the compile-time Translations check entirely.
